description: This email template is ideal for non-profit health and wellness organizations seeking a minimalistic, light blue and white design to share important news and tutorials related to Coronavirus (COVID-19) prevention and protection. The template uses a clean, sans serif font, ensuring easy readability. It is designed to address the ongoing COVID-19 crisis, providing a platform to share valuable information and updates to help stop the spread of the virus. The light tones convey a sense of calmness and positivity amid challenging times. This template is not just efficient, but also effective in delivering crucial messages to your audience, be it for events or general communication.
short_description: Ideal for non-profit health organizations, this minimalistic email template in light blue and white shares crucial COVID-19 prevention news and tutorials. With a clean, sans serif font for easy readability, it effectively communicates in these challenging times.

designer.description:
My name is Andrea Dall'Ara and I'm a freelancer digital graphic designer.
I'm focused on UI design for websites, emails and applications, logo and icon design and digital illustration.

html_data:
Coronavirus and Staying Healthy

Sed Steps to Prevent Illness, the best way to prevent illness is to avoid being exposed to COVID-19 from Centers from Disease Control and Prevention.

LEARN MORE

Watch for SYMPTOMS

Reported illnesses have ranged from mild symptoms to severe illness and death for confirmed coronavirus disease 2019 (COVID-19) cases.

The following symptoms may appear 2-14 days after exposure.*

FEVER

COUGH

SHORTNESS OF BREATH

 

TAKE STEPS TO PROTECT YOURSELF

Clean your hands often

Wash your hands often with soap and water for at least 20 seconds especially after you have been in a public place, or after blowing your nose, coughing, or sneezing. Avoid touching your eyes, nose, and mouth with unwashed hands.

Avoid close contact

Avoid close contact with people who are sick and put distance between yourself and other people if COVID-19 is spreading in your community. This is especially important for people who are at higher risk of getting very sick.

 

TAKE STEPS TO PROTECT OTHERS

Stay home if you’re sick

Cover coughs and sneezes

Wear a facemask if you are sick

Clean and disinfect

LEARN MORE →
 

What You Need To Know About Handwashing!

 
LEARN MORE →

All the informations comes from Centers from Disease Control and Prevention's official website. Stay up to date →

Lorem ipsum 2020© Medical
